sources.name: UCI Machine Learning Repository
sources.note: Classic tabular classification and regression datasets with clear metadata.

sources.name: OpenML
sources.note: Searchable dataset platform with API access and downloadable dataset metadata.

sources.name: Kaggle Datasets
sources.note: Large catalog, but many datasets require Kaggle account, API token, and license checks.

sources.name: AI Hub
sources.note: Korean public AI data portal. Many datasets require login and separate terms review.

datasets.name: Bank Marketing
datasets.why: Good for XGBoost because it mixes numeric and categorical features with realistic class imbalance.

datasets.name: Adult / Census Income
datasets.why: Familiar income-prediction task with categorical preprocessing, missing-value handling, and fairness discussion points.

datasets.name: Online Shoppers Purchasing Intention
datasets.why: Compact e-commerce conversion dataset that works well for a one-day student competition.

datasets.name: Bike Sharing
datasets.why: Time and weather features make feature engineering meaningful without requiring deep learning.

datasets.name: Wine Quality
datasets.why: Small, clean, and fast. Good for teaching baselines before moving to a larger final dataset.

datasets.name: Student Performance
datasets.why: Useful for a short practice round, though too small for a serious final leaderboard.

datasets.name: German Credit
datasets.why: A compact credit-risk task for validating the grader before the main event.

datasets.name: Phishing Websites
datasets.why: Balanced enough for quick experiments and large enough for tree-based model tuning.

datasets.name: Kaggle Tabular Classification catalog
datasets.why: Good fallback catalog if you want a domain-specific problem, but verify license and API access first.

datasets.name: AI Hub Korean public datasets
datasets.why: Best for a Korea-specific event. Budget time for account setup, terms, and preprocessing.
